text:
Once upon a time in the sleepy town of Willow Creek, lived a quiet squirrel named Oliver. He lived in an old oak tree at the edge of Whispering Woods, where he enjoyed the sun on his cheeks and the crunch of pine cones in his jaw.

One breezy autumn afternoon, Oliver noticed something strange: a group of children chasing each other, giggling with mischief. Intrigued, he sneaked closer and discovered their game—a relay race through the trees.

Oliver was quick! He scampered across branches, darted through spider webs, and slipped past bird nests with astonishing dexterity. By the time he reached the end, the kids were breathless, their laughter mingling with the rustle of leaves.

The next day, Oliver became the village's unwitting hero. The children left him sticky notes adorned with cheerful drawings, a token of appreciation for the speedy little squirrel. They called him "Quickie" and laughed whenever they saw him. Oliver was delighted, for how
